feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets (#16)

* feat(cli): ship Windows and GNU release targets

Summary:
- add Windows npm targets and GNU Linux release extras to the CLI
  packaging metadata and launcher fallback logic
- build real Windows self-host helper binaries in GitHub Actions and
  smoke test the packaged Windows tarballs on hosted runners
- enable packaged self-host runtime support on Windows with .exe
  resolution, shared process liveness checks, and stop markers

Rationale:
- the release pipeline only shipped the bundled self-host runtime for
  Unix targets, which left Windows builds without a usable helper
- we do not maintain a dedicated Windows builder, so GitHub Actions
  must produce and validate the Windows helper artifacts

Tests:
- turbo run check:fix
- bun run format
- bun run typecheck
- bun run lint --format json
- bun run test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(repo): split server integration into manual workflow

Summary:
- remove the slow server integration lane from the default CI workflow
  and its required results gate
- add a dedicated manual-only server integration workflow that keeps
  the existing Postgres setup and integration test command

Rationale:
- the integration lane is materially slower than the normal PR checks
  and should not run automatically on every change
- moving it to a separate workflow keeps the coverage available when
  maintainers need it without blocking regular CI

Tests:
- actionlint .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/server-integration.yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix Docker Codex bootstrap/runtime behavior (#77)

* feat(setup): clarify status prompts and local bootstrap

Summary:
- add per-prompt help text for interactive Project status mapping
- mirror the same explanations in the manual status mapping fallback
- add a local bootstrap make target and document how to use it

Rationale:
- the status mapping prompts were too terse for first-time setup users
- testing the remote bootstrap flow should not require merging to main first

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test setup::tests::
- make -n docker-bootstrap-local

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(make): proxy local bootstrap target at repo root

Summary:
- add a top-level make target that forwards docker-bootstrap-local
- preserve the rust makefile defaults by letting submake inherit command-line vars

Rationale:
- users naturally run the bootstrap test command from the repo root
- the root-level proxy removes an avoidable make target mismatch

Tests:
- make -n docker-bootstrap-local RECONFIGURE=1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docker): stabilize Codex bootstrap runtime

Summary:
- separate the bootstrap repo source from the managed workspace clone URL
- add local bootstrap helpers, setup guidance, and safer project-status copy
- harden Docker Codex runs with runtime caches, home-scoped skills, and quieter logs
- make workspace/bootstrap scripts preserve a usable default-branch baseline

Rationale:
- local bootstrap tests were cloning and operating on the wrong repository
- Docker workers were failing on missing tools, cache permissions, and noisy
  runtime errors instead of cleanly surfacing repo-level failures
- Codex needed stronger path and script guidance to avoid avoidable shell
  mistakes in unknown repositories

Tests:
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml config::tests::default_prompt_includes_repo_layout_guidance
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::filters_codex_telemetry_noise
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::downstream_build_diagnostics_are_debug_only
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::detects_error_like_stderr_lines
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::mod::tests::codex_repo_support_dirs_only_require_repo_owned_files
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::hook_commands_get_runtime_tool_cache_env_defaults
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::docker_workspace_scripts_exclude_seeded_support_dirs_from_git_status
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml orchestrator::tests::downgrades_expected_probe_failures
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::workflow_template_uses_env_placeholders
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::docker_workflow_describes_repo_owned_workspace_behavior
- local Docker rebuild and soak check via docker compose on kairastra-local-bootstrap

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ci): address clippy and bootstrap review follow-ups

Summary:
- replace the clippy-blocked iterator loop and async test env locking
- clear stale home-scoped Codex skills when the seed repo removes them
- switch bootstrap seed sync to rsync permission flags instead of recursive chmod
- narrow default-branch baseline fetches to the current and default branches

Rationale:
- PR #77 was failing rust-checks on clippy warnings introduced by the runtime
  and workspace test changes
- review comments flagged stale persisted skills and expensive seed volume
  permission walks in the Docker bootstrap path
- baseline setup only needs the active branch pair, not every remote branch

Tests:
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --check
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml config::tests::default_prompt_includes_repo_layout_guidance
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml providers::codex::runtime::tests::detects_error_like_stderr_lines
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml setup::tests::workflow_template_uses_env_placeholders
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml workspace::tests::docker_workspace_scripts_exclude_seeded_support_dirs_from_git_status
- cargo clippy --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(docker): migrate legacy workspace codex support

* fix(docker): remove legacy repo codex state

* fix(docker): chown overridden cache paths

---------

commit.message:
Harden relay stream reconnect on transient connect errors.

Retry transient connect/setup I/O failures during stream session
reconnect attempts within the existing hello retry window. This
stabilizes macOS reconnect behavior where connect setup can return
InvalidInput intermittently.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(auth): reuse refreshed Tipply sessions

are preserved across requests. Remove the manual 30s auth polling and
let the SDK handle request-driven and periodic refreshes internally.

commit.message:
refactor (#126)

* Add query language unification brainstorming document

Seven proposals for unifying emend's overlapping query languages
(selectors, patterns, GritQL-like queries, trace YAML, lint YAML,
policy YAML, Datalog): SQL-flavored, CSS selector extension, regex-for-ASTs,
jq/path-style, Datalog-first, minimal reduction, and EBNF productions.

Includes a coding-agent-friendly MCP query format section recommending
flat JSON with discriminated type fields.

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Rewrite query unification doc with synthesized proposals

Replace the mechanical "X-flavored version" proposals with 7 synthesized
designs that draw from multiple inspirations:

1. Pipe composition (jq + CSS pseudo-classes + Unix pipes)
2. List comprehensions (Python/Haskell + Rego)
3. S-expression core with surface sugar (Lisp + tree-sitter queries)
4. Prolog-style unification (CodeQL + Rego + pattern matching)
5. GraphQL-shaped queries over the code graph
6. Pragmatic "keep two, unify the rest" (merge YAML configs)
7. Cypher-inspired graph patterns (Neo4j + SPARQL)

Also adds cross-cutting analysis of structural vs relational query tension
and retains the LLM-agent-friendly MCP JSON format section.

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Add draft MCP server query language docs

Documents the proposed JSON-based MCP tool interface with tools for
find, lookup, replace, refs, flow, graph, deadcode, impact, datalog,
and batch operations. Includes parameter tables, examples, cookbook
patterns, and design principles.

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Revise MCP docs from 3-model critique; add synthesis to proposals

MCP docs revised based on feedback from Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus agents:
- Fix tool naming (emend_find headers vs "type":"find" examples)
- Standardize symbol/files param names across all tools
- Clarify kind vocabulary (AST node types vs semantic symbol kinds)
- Fix inside/not_inside to use pattern syntax, not globs
- Add Datalog relation schemas (column names and types)
- Document flow tool parameter interactions and metavar scoping
- Clarify transitive/depth, impact symbol/diff requirements
- Add batch error handling semantics
- Add output format cross-reference table
- Add "Not Yet Exposed" section for missing tools
- Fix design principles to match actual defaults

Proposals doc extended with synthesis from Opus agent:
- "Two Layers, One Rule Format" design
- Unified rules.yaml replacing 4 YAML schemas
- *code_match virtual Datalog relation bridging patterns to relational queries
- Implementation phases
- Novel ideas: rule extends, pattern-as-selector bridging, incremental eval

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Fix stale parameter names in MCP cookbook examples

- refs cookbook: filter -> ref_kind (matching tool spec)
- impact cookbook: diff -> diff_ref (matching tool spec)

Found by Haiku re-critique.

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Address remaining issues from second round of 3-model critique

- Clarify MCP tool naming vs batch "type" field dispatch
- Narrow dry-run statement to emend_replace only
- Fix emend_flow: from_pattern/to_pattern optionality with presets,
  effect/to_pattern mutual exclusivity, effect $VAR semantics
- Fix emend_impact: output default is "symbols" not "json"
- Fix emend_deadcode: output default to json, exclude_paths use glob syntax
- Split Output Format Reference into serialization formats vs scope selectors

Claude Code

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Implement MCP spec consensus: inline trace, symbol graph, deadcode params

- deadcode: add entry_point_decorators, entry_point_names, exclude_paths
  as direct parameters (override config file values)
- trace_analysis: add inline mode with from_pattern/to_pattern/not_through
  and preset= for framework rules, no config file needed
- graph: add symbol-level queries with direction/transitive/depth params
  alongside existing file-level mode
- Update mcp_app instructions to document new capabilities

[Claude Code]

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* Remove mcp-language.md proposal doc (superseded by implementation)

[Claude Code]

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BQcvApQFFJX2adeYqrHTgU

* docs: rewrite querying and MCP proposals (Codex)

* rules: support unified .emend/rules.yaml across lint/trace/policy (Codex)

* cli/mcp: implement canonical find and unified checks (Codex)

* cli: group edit/analyze/tool surfaces (Codex)

* refactor: split CLI into focused modules (Codex)

* fix: persist facts.db when bootstrapping fact graph (Codex)

* deadcode: detect unused modules and remove query parser (Codex)

* docs: update CLI docs for grouped commands (Codex)

* docs: replace roadmap with datalog migration phases (Codex)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: Python refactoring, search and linting cli.

commit.message:
chore(release): remove machine-specific windows test path

Summary:
- replace the tracked Administrator home path in the Windows runtime test
  fixture with a generic vault path
- keep the release safety preflight clear of machine-specific text while
  preserving the runtime-version assertions

Rationale:
- the local release dry-run correctly blocked on the tracked absolute path,
  so the repo needs a generic fixture before the release lane can proceed

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
- rg -n "/Users/|/home/|[A-Za-z]:\\\\Users\\\\" --glob '!node_modules' \
  --glob '!dist' --glob '!coverage' .

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(chatview): preserve chronology and hosted continuations

Summary:
- keep reasoning, tool calls, and content in emitted order during live
  rendering and persisted reloads
- remap repeated hosted tool-call ids per assistant occurrence so managed
  continuations bind later tool results to the correct round
- add desktop automation coverage and autoresearch evidence for the
  saved-chat cutoff repro and provider-parity proof

Rationale:
- saved managed chats were collapsing chronology on reload and could end in
  blank assistant tool-use rounds with no final answer content
- the fix needs durable live repro helpers and regression locks so this lane
  stays verifiable instead of returning to ad hoc QA

Tests:
- bash autoresearch.sh
- AUTORESEARCH_RUN_ID=20260330T200230Z bash autoresearch.checks.sh
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/services/__tests__/SystemSculptService.test.ts
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/testing/automation/__tests__/DesktopAutomationBridge.test.ts
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/client.test.mjs
- node scripts/jest.mjs --config jest.config.cjs --runInBand \
  --runTestsByPath src/views/chatview/__tests__/input-handler-tool-loop.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(worker): expand archive fixture coverage

Add a native test for archive entry normalization, junk filtering, and natural path ordering.

Expand archive fixtures and functional coverage for:
- single root folder layouts
- Unicode folder and page names
- ComicInfo and junk sidecars
- multifolder archives with natural ordering

Fix worker-side Unicode handling found by the new matrix:
- initialize a UTF-8 locale in the archive worker
- accept libarchive warning headers during entry scans
- prefer wide-path names for RAR entries

Validated with:
- ctest --test-dir native/worker/build --output-on-failure
- bash test/workers/test-archive-worker.sh --worker native/worker/build/comiscopio-worker --fixtures test/fixtures/archive

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(setup): tailor GitHub token guidance for project ownership (#76)

Summary:
- reorder projects_v2 setup so project URL parsing happens before token entry
- render GitHub token help differently for user-owned, org-owned, and unknown Projects v2
- align setup docs with the new fine-grained-vs-classic PAT guidance

Rationale:
- users were looking for fine-grained Projects permissions before setup had enough context to explain why they were missing
- showing ownership-aware guidance at token entry reduces confusion without adding a new prompt

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test setup::tests::
- cargo test (fails in providers::claude::runtime::tests::reads_stdout_while_large_prompt_is_still_being_written)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
release(plugin): prepare 5.3.2 candidate

Summary:
- preserve completed local Pi tool-call state in ChatView so Pi tool
  executions do not get rerouted through the hosted tool loop
- pin Studio Pi auth inventory to the bundled auth-storage path and
  expand macOS and Windows provider-connected coverage on
  openai/gpt-5.4-mini
- harden Windows native release prep with runtime-version refresh,
  redacted bootstrap diagnostics, updated release notes, and 5.3.2
  version metadata

Rationale:
- keep canonical Pi auth and tool-calling paths strict instead of
  masking breakage behind fallbacks
- make the Windows release lane reproducible and future-agent friendly
  so stale runtimes and transient host noise stop causing trial and
  error
- capture the actual 5.3.2 release evidence in-tree before any publish
  step

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/bootstrap.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-desktop-automation.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-clean-install-parity.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/remote-run.test.mjs
- bash autoresearch.sh
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run check:plugin
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:desktop:provider-connected
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:windows:provider-connected
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks git . --log-opts='5.3.1..HEAD'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
